description: Adapted from the STOP-BANG clinical screening questionnaire (Chung et al., 2008) for population-level risk estimation. Uses district-level proxy indicators instead of individual measurements. NOT a diagnostic tool — designed for screening camp prioritization.
citation: Chung F, et al. STOP questionnaire: a tool to screen patients for OSA. Anesthesiology. 2008;108(5):812-821.
asian_adjustments: BMI threshold lowered from 35 to 25 kg/m² per Asian population guidelines (WHO Expert Consultation, 2004).

factors.name: Pressure (Hypertension)
factors.rationale: Hypertension is the strongest STOP-BANG correlate with OSA at population level

factors.name: BMI (Obesity)
factors.rationale: Obesity (BMI ≥ 25 for Asian populations) is the #1 modifiable risk factor for OSA

factors.name: Age > 50
factors.rationale: OSA prevalence increases significantly after age 50

factors.name: Gender (Male)
factors.rationale: Male sex is an independent risk factor (2-3x higher prevalence)

factors.name: Air Quality (PM2.5)
factors.rationale: Chronic air pollution exposure aggravates upper airway inflammation and OSA severity (Billings et al., 2019)

omitted_factors.name: Snoring
omitted_factors.reason: No population-level proxy; used as awareness signal instead

omitted_factors.name: Tired
omitted_factors.reason: No population-level proxy available in public datasets

omitted_factors.name: Neck circumference
omitted_factors.reason: No population-level anthropometric data at district level

awareness_source: Google Trends search interest for 'sleep apnea' + 'snoring' + 'CPAP' by state
